meta.title: My Covid Sites
meta.tagline: A collection of websites documenting Covid-19 vaccine issues, regulatory oversight, and the people impacted.

sites.title: 248 Questions
sites.description: A website presenting 248 scientific questions for the FDA regarding COVID-19 vaccine manufacturing, safety testing, and regulatory oversight, based on published work by researchers David Wiseman, PhD, and Maria Gutschi, PharmD.

sites.title: Anecdotals (the movie)
sites.description: Support site for the documentary movie "Anecdotals," which follows people whose lives were dramatically altered by COVID-19 vaccine adverse reactions—individuals who found themselves censored, dismissed, and caught between a polarized debate, unable to get medical care or acknowledgment.

sites.title: Christine Cotton Archive
sites.description: A bilingual (English/French) preservation archive of the work of Christine Cotton, the French biostatistician who critiqued Pfizer's C4591001 clinical trial on Good Clinical Practice grounds. It includes her reports and book, the captured text of her major works, an indexed catalog filterable by type, tag, and language, her 18 video interviews (with translations and summaries), and the 231 external sources she cited.

sites.title: Committee Comments
sites.description: Full-text search of public comments submitted in advance of the canceled February 25-27, 2026 Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP) meeting. Offers improved usability over regulations.gov, including a checkbox to surface comments about vaccine injury.

sites.title: Covid Documents
sites.description: FOIA'd documents from PHMPT.org, filterable by tags, modules, company, etc. Includes metadata and a searchable data dictionary. Limited to Pfizer and Moderna Adult EUA and Pfizer Adult BLA documents.

sites.title: Covid Documents — FOIA (full set)
sites.description: Companion site to CovidDocuments.com, including ALL the PHMPT.org documents released so far, but doesn't include the document tagging or metadata.

sites.title: Covista — Tri-State Alliance
sites.description: COVID Vaccine-Injured Support — Tri-State Alliance: an independent, non-partisan group for COVID-19 vaccine-injured individuals in New Jersey, New York, and Pennsylvania, working to secure acknowledgment of vaccine injuries, access to healthcare, and compensation programs.

sites.title: Drop The Case
sites.description: Dr. Ron Elfenbein is a Maryland frontline COVID doctor convicted of healthcare fraud for billing practices that a federal judge later ruled were lawful. In a 93-page acquittal opinion consistent with the government's own pandemic-era guidance, the judge cleared him. But the DOJ appealed, and Dr. Elfenbein faces a new trial in August 2026.

sites.title: Good Nutrition is Antiviral
sites.description: An annotated, science-backed compilation of nutrition research showing that produce, spices, and beans have antiviral properties, while a poor diet increases vulnerability to infection. Public health messaging on COVID-19 failed to communicate this. Published March 2020.

sites.title: V-Safety Signals
sites.description: Features full-text search of a Senate PSI interim report on COVID-19 vaccine safety signal masking; includes clickable footnotes, PDF deep-links, a timeline, and a glossary.
